title: lepidagathis rajasekharae
intro: Lepidagathis rajasekharae, a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae, is a rare and endemic plant found in the Western Ghats of India. It is known for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and skin conditions, and has also been found to possess hepatoprotective and cardio protective properties. Lepidagathis rajasekharae is often prepared as a herbal tea or decoction, and its extracts are used in various pharmaceutical and cosmetic applications.
